container.title: Rules
container.description: In choosing your units, you must ensure that you complete no more than 10 units (60 credit points) at Level 1 and at least six units (36 credit points) at Level 3, including four units (24 credit points) at Level 3 offered by the Faculty of Business and Economics.

container.title: Part D. Elective studies

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Part D. Elective studies

container.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of elective study. Electives may be selected from within the Bachelor of Commerce. These may also be used to complete a second major (as listed above) or one or more minors (as listed below). 

Elective units can also be chosen from across the University, including to complete a major or minor from another course, as long as you have the prerequisites and there are no restrictions on enrolling in the units. You should also be aware of travel and timetabling limitations.

Notes:
- Enrolment in Faculty of Business and Economics units at campuses other than the campus of enrolment is subject to there being capacity after students from that campus have enrolled.

- If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards this Commerce degree.

container.title: Part A. Core studies
Specified commerce elective
container.description:
You must complete 6 credit points (one elective unit) from the Faculty of Business and Economics

You can enrich your degree to hone your academic and professional skills with a range of Flagship Rich Educational Experiences (FREE), including the Research, Experimentation and Discovery, Monash Innovation Guarantee, Global Immersion Guarantee and Volunteering. These flagship rich educational experience units may be credited in place of your specified commerce elective if approved by the faculty, or alternatively utilising your elective studies in Part D. There are both 6 and 12 credit point unit options available.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Core studies

container.description:
You must complete 42 credit points, comprising:
- 36 credit points (six units) from the following list; and
- 6 credit points (one unit) from specified commerce electives.

Notes:
- You must complete either ACC1100 or ACC1001 (one accounting unit).
- If you intend to complete a major in Accounting or Actuarial Studies, you must complete ACC1100.
- If you are undertaking the Bachelor of Laws (Honours) and Bachelor of Commerce course (L3005), BTC1110 in Part A must be replaced with a Commerce elective.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: Part C. Capstone studies

container.description:
You must complete one unit (6, 12 or 24 credit points) from the Faculty’s capstone portfolio of units which includes capstone units, consulting projects, international experiences and internships (industry placements).

Notes:
- If you choose a 12 or 24 credit point unit it will reduce the amount of points available for electives in Part D which may impact on your ability to complete a major using electives.

- If you are completing a major in Accounting and seeking professional accounting accreditation you need to complete ACX3900. You can undertake more than one unit from the following lists, which will count towards your electives.

container.title: Part B. Major studies
container.description:
You must complete at least one major from the list below. Depending on the major chosen, you may have already studied one of the required units as part of your core studies. If so, you must complete an additional commerce unit to bring the combined commerce study to at least 96 credit points from the Faculty of Business and Economics.

Notes:
- No more than 12 credit points can be credited towards two majors, or a major and a minor, within a course. The same credit points cannot be credited towards more than one minor.

-If you are intending to undertake an honours year, you should ensure you complete the specific units required for honours in your chosen major area of study.
